# Observability Checklist: Logging and Debugging

**Purpose**: Validate completeness, clarity, and consistency of logging, error handling, and observability requirements
**Created**: 2026-04-07
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 - Are logging level semantics (`error`, `warn`, `info`, `debug`, `trace`) defined with unambiguous boundary criteria between adjacent levels? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001] — Defined in FR-001 (constitution Principle VII)
- [x] CHK002 - Are structured logging field requirements (`package`, `operation_id`, `duration_ms`) defined for all operation types, not just a sample? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004] — FR-003 now specifies minimum fields per operation type
- [x] CHK003 - Are requirements defined for which modules/functions are in scope for `#[tracing::instrument]` beyond "all public async functions"? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003] — FR-003 updated: public async + sync with I/O; private helpers excluded
- [x] CHK004 - Are requirements specified for log output format consistency (structured JSON vs human-readable) across CLI and GUI? [Gap] — Out of scope: existing infrastructure (spec 015/016) already defines formats
- [x] CHK005 - Is the scope of "I/O, network, database, and process paths" in FR-009 enumerated or bounded with inclusion/exclusion criteria? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009] — FR-009 updated with explicit in-scope/out-of-scope lists
- [x] CHK006 - Are requirements defined for frontend logging utility method signatures (context parameter, severity levels, output target)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019] — FR-019 updated: context string + message string, writes to LogPanel store

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 - Is "at least 80% reduction" in SC-003 measurable against a defined baseline count, and is the method for measuring specified? [Clarity, Spec §SC-003] — SC-003 updated: baseline measured by rg before work begins, scoped to FR-009 files
- [x] CHK008 - Is "meaningful failure" in FR-011 (`let _ =` patterns) quantified or defined with examples? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-011] — FR-011 updated with definition: data loss, resource leak, broken user expectation + examples
- [x] CHK009 - Is "critical paths" in FR-012 (`.ok()` patterns) bounded with specific criteria? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-012] — FR-012 updated with explicit critical vs non-critical path lists
- [x] CHK010 - Is "relevant context fields" in FR-003 specified with a minimum set? [Clarity, Spec §FR-003] — FR-003 updated: operations=operation_id+package, network=url+duration_ms, file=path
- [x] CHK011 - Is "key user actions" in FR-020 enumerated or defined with inclusion criteria? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-020] — FR-020 updated: route nav, operation buttons, settings saves. Search keystrokes excluded.
- [x] CHK012 - Is "within 2 seconds" in SC-001 measured from error occurrence or from user action initiation? [Clarity, Spec §SC-001] — SC-001 updated: measured from backend error to toast display

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK013 - Are log level assignments consistent between backend requirements and constitution? [Consistency] — FR-001 defines canonical levels; FR-003-008 use same levels consistently
- [x] CHK014 - Is the anti-duplication principle consistent with FR-013/FR-014? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013] — FR-013/FR-014 updated: explicitly state boundary-only logging, not duplicating core
- [x] CHK015 - Are error notification requirements consistent between FR-016 and FR-015? [Consistency] — FR-016 updated: mutation callback takes precedence, boundary MUST NOT duplicate
- [x] CHK016 - Is `unwrap()` treatment consistent between FR-009 and FR-010? [Consistency] — Categories are disjoint: I/O/network/DB/process vs mutex/const/regex/test

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK017 - Can SC-002 be objectively measured? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002] — SC-002 updated: verified by 3 representative failure scenarios with operation_id correlation
- [x] CHK018 - Can SC-004 be verified systematically? Is adjacency defined? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004] — SC-004 updated: "within 3 lines" or inline code comment
- [x] CHK019 - Can SC-005 be objectively verified? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005] — SC-005 updated: verifiable by reading docs and checking pass/fail judgment capability
- [x] CHK020 - Is SC-007 a hard count or actual count? [Clarity, Spec §SC-007] — SC-007 says "all GUI command handlers" — tracks actual count, not hardcoded 19

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK021 - Are requirements defined for startup/shutdown logging? [Coverage, Gap] — Added to Edge Cases: stderr fallback before init, flush on shutdown
- [x] CHK022 - Are requirements defined for disk-full log rotation failure? [Coverage, Edge Case] — Added to Edge Cases: degrades to stderr-only, no crash
- [x] CHK023 - Are requirements defined for concurrent operation logging? [Coverage, Gap] — Added FR-008a: unique operation_id per top-level operation, propagates to child spans
- [x] CHK024 - Are requirements for LogPanel render capacity specified? [Coverage, Edge Case] — Existing 1000-entry cap handles this; LogPanel already truncates oldest
- [x] CHK025 - Are requirements defined for sensitive data exclusion? [Coverage, Security Gap] — Added FR-012a: passwords and tokens excluded; user paths acceptable (local-only logging)

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK026 - Is performance impact defined for tight-loop instrumentation? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-006] — FR-006 updated: tight loops use event macros, not per-call span creation
- [x] CHK027 - Are signature change requirements defined for unwrap removal? [Edge Case] — Already in Edge Cases section
- [x] CHK028 - Is error storm behavior defined for Vue error boundary? [Edge Case, Spec §FR-015] — FR-015 updated: rate-limit max 3 toasts per 5 seconds, all still logged
- [x] CHK029 - Are frontend logging requirements defined for dev mode without Tauri? [Edge Case] — Already in Edge Cases section

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK030 - Is "no new dependencies" assumption validated? [Assumption] — tracing 0.1 with existing features covers all needs
- [x] CHK031 - Is toast provider availability validated? [Assumption] — PrimeVue toast is app-level provider, available in all components
- [x] CHK032 - Is LogPanel 1000-entry cap adequate? [Assumption] — Adequate for debug; trace volume at full verbosity may truncate but that's acceptable behavior (noted in Edge Cases)
- [x] CHK033 - Are cross-spec dependencies documented? [Dependency, Gap] — Added to Assumptions: builds on spec 015 (CLI tracing) and spec 016 (GUI FrontendLogLayer)

## Notes

- All 33 items resolved on 2026-04-07
- 4 items required user input (CHK021 startup logging, CHK023 operation_id correlation, CHK025 sensitive data, CHK028 error storms)
- 14 items resolved by tightening spec language (ambiguity/clarity fixes)
- 15 items were already passing (clear in original spec)
